triage: 3-worker fleet (routine, well-specified UI task on frozen engine contract)

workers.focus: Focus on test rigor and edge cases.
Focus on accessibility and faithfulness to the spec's display contracts.

divergence: footprints identical (one extra test file in one candidate); content divergence high; no round-2 trigger

review.defects_note: both contract/tips-level, ZERO behavioral bugs (both prior runs' UI rounds shipped one); B: main() signature deviation (new_game_fn param its own tests depend on); C: message-constants violation (inline literals in source, re-typed substrings in tests)
review.seed_tip_audit: argparse seed entry (N/A in round 1) became applicable and was honored by all three candidates — the try/except SystemExit defect that shipped in BOTH prior projects' UI rounds did not occur

verdict.reasoning: A is faithful to the display contracts (SPEC 9/10/18): correct render order, progress-driven five-stage growth tested at every stage, label+count accessibility with weeds as a standalone count, whole-game pure-ASCII verified including end screens, argparse exit codes intact, EOF/interrupt goodbyes clean, and the strongest named-constant discipline of the field with positive forced-scenario e2e tests. B has the largest suite (145) and genuinely strong accessibility assertions, but deviates from the brief's REQUIRED main(argv, input_fn, output_fn) signature by adding new_game_fn — and its own e2e tests depend on the extra parameter; an injection path without a signature change existed (patching module attributes), so the deviation was avoidable and is disqualifying against an explicit contract. C is fully signature-compliant and rigorous in volume (119) but commits the field's heaviest tips violation: no named constants for its own UI strings and tests that re-type engine message literals as substrings despite the briefing's define-once-and-import rule, the exact rot pattern two prior rounds distilled against. A's only blemishes are minor: one win-screen body line asserted as a literal, and keyword-only I/O params (a benign strictness).
